channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti on bin and black cabinet, 31st March
item.description:
Pen tags on both the green public bin attached to lamppost, and large black control unit for traffic lights outside Harris Primary Academy Kent House school.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overcrowded, 31st March
item.description:
Bike need collection

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Warren Street, Fitzrovia, Greater London

item.title: Street signs vandalised, 31st March
item.description:
There is graffiti (pen tags) on both street signs for Ravenscroft Rd, where it meets Beckenham Road. Includes offensive language.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravenscroft Road, Penge, Greater London

item.title: Overcrowded bike, 31st March
item.description:
Bike on publice street over crowded

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Warren Street, Fitzrovia, Greater London

item.title: Decrepit phone booth, 31st March
item.description:
Not working and covered in graffiti. Could be removed entirely.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glade Path, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole, 31st March
item.description:
This potholes has been there for over 3 months, damaging my car everyday. There are bus lanes each side of the road and no space to escape these potholes

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 151, Cricklewood Broadway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent: Bin Collection and Hygiene Issues at Hazel Walk, 31st March
item.description:
There has been an ongoing issue with missed and insufficient bin collections outside One Stop Hazel Walk. This is now significantly affecting the shop environment, as overflowing bins are attracting pests that can potentially enter the store. Additionally, residents from nearby flats are disposing of general waste and dog waste around the bin area, leading to an unhygienic and unpleasant situation. The surrounding ground and bin area are becoming damaged and increasingly unsanitary. It appears that the current collection frequency and schedule are not adequate to manage the volume of waste. This matter requires urgent attention to maintain cleanliness, hygiene, and a safe environment for both locals, customers and staff.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hazel Walk, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead cat, 31st March
item.description:
Found dead cat on halcot. Assuming it had been hit by a car and a the foxes are starting to eat it
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Halcot Avenue, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 31st March
item.description:
Canisters & general rubbish fly tipped. Too heavy for me to carry away to a bin.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glengall Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned garden waste bin, 31st March
item.description:
The bin has been here for several months
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Braemar Avenue, Barnehurst, Greater London

item.title: Broken glass, 31st March
item.description:
The glass has been here for two weeks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Braemar Avenue, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Risk to pedestrians from cyclists cutting across them crossing on a ´green man’., 31st March
item.description:
While crossing Shad Thames on foot, East to West on Jamaica Rd, I was nearly run over by a cyclist cutting across me. I pointed out it was a ‘green man’ and the cyclist said it was for him too. Not believing this could be the case, I observed a complete traffic light cycle on my return journey and was surprised to see he was correct. It is dangerous to have a simultaneous ´green man’ for pedestrians crossing a street and cyclists turning right into it. I have never encountered this before. Please consider staggering the green lights or having clear signage mandating pedestrian priority. I don’t know how long this situation has existed, sorry. Thanks for your kind attention.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tooley Street, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wire fencing and broken fences on pathway and parts of pathway, 31st March
item.description:
King John's walk. All overgrown with wire under overgrown weeds . Wrapped around tree. All fencing broken. Loads of dog waste in bags. Cans etc. Is this a public footpath or private land only its very dangerous for dog walkers with all this wire everywhere. King John's walk from a20 towards kipperton drive

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): West Hallowes, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 87 Salusbury Road, 31st March
item.description:
A fridge freezer has been dumped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salusbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: Missed bin collections of all waste. Food and general and paper and glass, 31st March
item.description:
All the bins are full. The food bin was not emptied last week now all bins full and unemptied
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Overbury Avenue, Beckenham, Greater London

item.title: Dumped stuff, 31st March

item.title: Fly tipping.... Again! Aren't we all fed up with this?, 31st March
item.description:
Every week this corner looks like this. What preventative measures are in place to stop this? Charge more to everyone else with council tax, to clean up the mess of others? How about monitoring the situation with cameras, or have they been cut down?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Filey Avenue, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The grass outside number 10 needs cutting., 31st March
item.description:
The grass outside number 10 needs cutting.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 10, Grasmere Road, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fridge dumped outside our flat, 31st March
item.description:
A fridge has been dumped in the parking space outside our flat since last night 30/3

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Barnabas Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish everywhere, 31st March
item.description:
The only bin available in this area has been full for days and no collection . Rubbish is now on the floor. All this area is getting worse and worse, i can see increase number of rats/mice which is not ideal for food shops next door.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kidbrooke Park Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet
